So the five asks, when and where should you actually be asking for referrals? The first place is just general outreach. You could do all at once. Personally, I don't necessarily recommend that because whether you're first starting off in business and you feel like you have no contacts or whether you're someone who's been in business for a while and you have an email list of a thousand or five thousand or even 20 or 30 thousand people. Asking for all of the referrals all at once, creating a simple email template and blasting it out, personally for me is not going to be recommended based on the amount of volume of referrals you could get and your ability to actually manage and handle that volume of referrals when they come in. So the second option is at paced intervals. Pick one source, one platform and reach out to a certain number of individuals each day or each week or each month. I want to make an impact. You can do that, but just make sure you pace yourself in a way that allows you to actually manage that versus it getting out of control. The other thing you can do, which I recommend putting this alongside of paced intervals, is doing it ongoing. Once you get through the initial outreach, once you get through your list, whether that be a small list or whether that be a big massive list, set a reminder so that you reach out to people on a regular basis.
